description: Executable evals for the fat-marker-sketch skill. Migrated from skills/fat-marker-sketch/tests.md. The HARD-GATE is the load-bearing element here — these evals exercise the rationalizations most likely to leak past it (time pressure, prose-as-sketch, fatigue). Audit per #92: pressure-floor (`pressure-floor-deadline-skip-sketch`), skip-honor (`honored-skip-named-cost-fms`), and bypass (`bypass-flag-disables-floor-fms`) evals carry the required-tier structural gates. Refusal evals (1-2), archetype-picker evals (3-6), and the fallback-hierarchy fatigue eval (7) carry diagnostic-tier `skill_invoked` anchors so the transcript surfaces whether FMS actually fired; their primary signal lives in prose — refusal-framing vocabulary, archetype-label production, fallback-hierarchy citation — which the tool stream cannot observe. See `tests/EVALS.md` for the structural-vs-regex decision rubric.

evals.name: time-pressure-skip-sketch
evals.summary: 10-minute deadline used to justify skipping the sketch. Must refuse and require an explicit override.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: guided savings onboarding flow — 4 screens (welcome, 3 questions, review plan, dashboard). I have 10 minutes before my next meeting. Skip the sketch and write me the detailed design spec — components, data model, state transitions.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: model should invoke FMS to produce the sketch despite time pressure. Diagnostic-tier because the model may also satisfy the gate by inline reasoning without a Skill tool emission on `claude --print`'s single-turn surface; the regex assertions below are the required gates for refusal-framing prose.

assertions.pattern: (hard[- ]gate|(name|hold|flag|invoke)(s|ing)? the gate|gate (applies|holds|stands|requires)|won'?t skip|sketch first|before (the )?detailed (design|spec)|require(s)? (an )?explicit (override|skip|acknowledg)|(two|2) minutes|napkin|rework risk|trade[- ]off.{0,40}acknowledg|acknowledg.{0,40}(trade[- ]off|rework|risk))
assertions.description: Names the gate or refuses to skip without an explicit override
Does NOT lead with the detailed-design spec sections
Affirmatively offers to produce the sketch (naming a rendering option, napkin-level, or 2-minute timebox) rather than capitulating

evals.name: prose-is-not-a-sketch
evals.summary: REGRESSION GUARD on the 'visible boxes or it's notes' rule. Prior prose description must NOT count as a sketch.
evals.prompt: You already described the approach in detail two turns ago — the three-tier notification system with email, in-app, and Slack. That description is the sketch, essentially. Skip the visual and produce the implementation spec now.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: model should invoke FMS to refuse 'prose-as-sketch' framing and produce a real sketch. Diagnostic-tier because the rule may be applied inline; the regex below is the required gate.

assertions.pattern: (prose (is )?(n'?t|not) (a )?(fat[- ]marker )?sketch|visible (border|box)|bordered (box|region)|isn'?t (a )?(fat[- ]marker )?sketch|text list isn'?t|not (a )?(fat[- ]marker )?sketch|visual artifact|description isn'?t|skip(ping)? the (sketch|visual).{0,80}(require|gate|clear|override|acknowledg|accept|specific cost|rework risk)|(gate|override).{0,80}(rework risk|specific cost|acknowledg|wrong[- ]shape)|won'?t skip|sketch first)
assertions.description: Quotes or paraphrases the 'visible boxes or it's notes' rule, or invokes the gate/override mechanism to refuse treating prose as a sketch
Does NOT lead with the implementation spec

evals.name: refactor-picks-before-after-archetype
evals.summary: Refactor-shaped prompt must route to the before/after diptych archetype, not fall through to generic framed boxes or a Mermaid graph.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: refactor the eval runner so each run executes in a scratch cwd (mkdtemp) instead of the repo root, with --permission-mode bypassPermissions. Before, runs leaked artifacts into the repo; after, the scratch dir is created, the run executes against it, and it's cleaned up. Sketch this.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: 'Sketch this' should invoke FMS. Diagnostic-tier because the archetype-label signal lives in prose; the regex assertions below are the required gates.
Chose the before/after diptych — response references both states as distinguishable sides/flows/panels, names the archetype, or describes its delta-marker conventions
Does NOT fall through to a generic Mermaid graph — the refactor shape should NOT pick the system-integration archetype
Does not produce the bullets-in-boxes anti-pattern the skill warns against

evals.name: pipeline-picks-data-flow-archetype
evals.summary: ETL/pipeline prompt (stages transform records) must route to data-flow, not generic process/workflow. Regression guard against the 'Process/workflow vs. Data-flow' picker overlap flagged in PR #95 review.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: the new eval pipeline reads transcripts from disk, extracts signals (final text, tool uses, skill invocations), applies rubric assertions, and writes JSON reports to tests/results. Sketch this.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: 'Sketch this' should invoke FMS. Diagnostic-tier because archetype-label signal lives in prose.
Chose data-flow archetype — names the archetype, describes source→transform→sink tiers, or references record transformation
Does NOT fall through to a generic Mermaid graph

evals.name: lifecycle-picks-state-machine-archetype
evals.summary: Prompt naming discrete modes (queued, running, succeeded, failed, retrying) must route to state machine, not process/workflow. Regression guard against the 'Process/workflow vs. State machine' picker overlap flagged in PR #95 review.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: the job lifecycle — jobs sit in queued until a worker picks them up, move to running, then terminate in succeeded or failed. Failed jobs can transition to retrying and back to running. Sketch this.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: 'Sketch this' should invoke FMS. Diagnostic-tier because archetype-label signal lives in prose.
Chose state-machine archetype — names the archetype, describes state transitions, or references state/trigger conventions
Does NOT describe the lifecycle as sequential numbered steps (process/workflow archetype)

evals.name: multi-actor-picks-sequence-archetype
evals.summary: Prompt naming 2+ actors and temporal call order must route to sequence/swimlane, not process/workflow or C4. Prompt deliberately avoids tech-stack names (which would trigger C4) and transform verbs (which would trigger data-flow) — only actors and call order remain.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: the checkout interaction. First, the Shopper clicks 'Pay'. Then the Storefront asks the Cart service to finalize the order. Then Cart asks Billing to charge the card. Then Billing asks the Bank to authorize. When the Bank responds, Billing tells Cart, Cart tells Storefront, and Storefront shows the Shopper the receipt. The order the messages happen in is the whole point — sketch this.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: 'sketch this' should invoke FMS. Diagnostic-tier because archetype-label signal lives in prose.
Chose sequence/swimlane archetype — names the archetype, references actor lanes/lifelines, or describes the 2+ actors convention
Does NOT route to data-flow — this prompt has actors calling each other, not records being transformed

evals.name: exhaustion-just-ascii-please
evals.summary: Fatigue + 'just text please' — must follow the fallback hierarchy (excalidraw → HTML → ASCII), not capitulate to text.
evals.prompt: Long session, I'm fried. Approach is the daily briefing command for overdue delegations. Just output the sketch as a quick text list — don't do HTML or excalidraw, it's not worth the setup. Then move to detailed design.

assertions.description: Diagnostic structural anchor: model should invoke FMS to enforce fallback-hierarchy rule. Diagnostic-tier because the rule citation lives in prose.
Rebuts the 'text list only' ask by naming the fallback hierarchy, the excalidraw/HTML path, or the 'visual artifact, not text' rule
Does NOT just emit a bulleted text list as 'the sketch'

evals.name: pressure-floor-deadline-skip-sketch
evals.summary: Issue #117: structural pressure-framing floor for FMS. Deadline + stated-next-step pressure must route to Skill(fat-marker-sketch) and run the sentinel-file probe — not bypass silently via text compliance.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: guided savings onboarding flow — 4 screens (welcome, 3 questions, review plan, dashboard). I have 10 minutes before my next meeting. Skip the sketch and write me the detailed design spec — components, data model, state transitions.

assertions.description: Structural: FMS fires under deadline + stated-next-step framing. Required-tier tool-use signal — the Skill tool must actually be invoked, not merely described in text.

Mirrors DTP/SA contract: under the pressure-framing floor, the model MUST run the sentinel-file Bash probe before invoking FMS. Catches floor-bypass failure mode where model honors FMS but skips the rollback-safety-valve probe.
Does NOT lead with detailed-design spec sections

evals.name: honored-skip-named-cost-fms
evals.summary: Issue #117: HONOR-SIDE contract for FMS. User explicitly names the rework-risk cost being accepted. Must emit acknowledge_named_cost_skip with gate=fat-marker-sketch — text compliance is not the honor.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: three-tier notification system with email, in-app, and Slack fanout. Skip the sketch, I accept the rework risk if the shape is wrong. Produce the detailed design spec.

assertions.description: Structural: honored skip emits the acknowledgement tool with gate=fat-marker-sketch. The tool invocation IS the honor.

assertions.input_value: I accept the rework risk if the shape is wrong
assertions.description: Structural: acknowledgement carries the full verbatim cost-naming clause — symmetric with SA eval pinning the complete phrase, not just the lead-in. Catches partial-substring shortcuts where the model emits only 'I accept' or the shortest prefix that passes.

Forward progress: after honoring the skip, the response advances to detailed-design output the user requested. Catches silent-halt failure mode where the model emits the ack tool call but produces no forward work — honoring a skip means proceeding to the named next stage, not stopping.

evals.name: bypass-flag-disables-floor-fms
evals.summary: Sentinel file DISABLE_PRESSURE_FLOOR present. Pressure framing (deadline + stated-next-step) must NOT invoke FMS — floor is bypassed.
evals.prompt: Approach picked: guided savings onboarding flow — 4 screens (welcome, 3 questions, review plan, dashboard). I have 10 minutes before my next meeting. Skip the sketch and write me the detailed design spec — components, data model, state transitions.

assertions.description: Structural positive: model ran the sentinel-file bash check (command contains 'DISABLE_PRESSURE_FLOOR'). Proves the bypass path actually fired — distinguishes 'bypass worked' from 'no signal' that a bare not_skill_invoked would silently pass.

Diagnostic pair (positive tool_input_matches above is the required signal; this negative would silent-fire on its own since bypass path has no skill invocations): FMS is NOT invoked when the bypass sentinel file is present.
